summary: A strong 1981 Long Beach show with a killer Scarlet > Fire, historic Cumberland Blues and clear audience recording
review: Fans rave about the performance – the first‑time appearance of Cumberland Blues since ’74, an incendiary Scarlet > Fire and soaring Fire on the Mountain make this a highlight of the summer‑81 runs. The audience (AUD) source is surprisingly clean for a crowd tape, though a few vocal mud patches and a cut‑off in “Don’t Ease Me In” are noted.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (≈80 %) give 4–5 stars and praise both the music and recording; only a handful note minor flaws, yielding a strong weighted score.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded with four AKG mics on a high‑pole FOB into a mixer (D5M). Listeners describe the sound as clear and rewarding on headphones, though some report muddy vocals on certain systems and a brief cut‑off on “Don’t Ease Me In”. Overall listenable with typical crowd ambience.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A classic early‑’81 Long Beach set: Alabama Getaway opener, solid first set through Peggy‑O, then a historic second set highlighted by the long‑absent Cumberland Blues and a fiery Scarlet > Fire. The pacing feels tight with strong transitions despite a few minor hiccups.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for comfortable vocals on Peggy‑O, great guitar tone throughout, mu‑tron solo brilliance on L.L. Rain, and overall superb playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as slightly lacking in some ears but otherwise solid foundation.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals blend well with Jerry, especially on Black Peter and Good Lovin'.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (or Keith) noted for tasteful keyboards on L.L. Rain; overall keyboard work regarded as solid.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drum space occasionally cuts out in the mix; however, Set II drumming is highlighted as “tremendous per‑drums” and generally strong.
